title: Task
description: A Task is the atomic reusable unit of human activity: a Role performing exactly one Capability. A Task reads as '<Role> does <Capability>' (e.g. 'ClosingSpecialist does Loan.Close'). Different Capability = different Task. Tasks are referenced by Steps inside a Process, and are the primary unit of composition for SOPs.

name.description: The kebab-case stable identifier for this task.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of what this task accomplishes and why.

role.description: The Role (PascalCase) that performs this task. Must reference a declared Role.

capability.description: The Capability (Resource.Action) this task invokes. Must reference a declared Capability. One Task = one Capability.

domain.description: The dot-separated domain path this task is scoped to.

examples.name: close-loan
examples.description: Closing specialist closes an approved loan, preparing it for disbursement.
